content.claim: 73% of SEN students (approximately 51,000) are educated in integrated (mainstream) settings rather than segregated special schools, yet institutions and specialists face 'increasingly large challenges' in providing integrated education. Teacher surveys report 83.6% of respondents observed increases in students requiring developmental support, and research indicates placement decisions are shaped as much by institutional resource capacity and structure as by student characteristics.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: 'Challenges' and strain are reported qualitatively; no quantified metric of resource adequacy per integrated SEN student exists in cited sources
teacher survey on developmental support needs does not distinguish between SEN-classified and unclassified students requiring support
causality between resource capacity and placement outcomes requires longitudinal institutional-level data not present in retrieved sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
